name: ECANet-Skew Dataset
description: s-Skewed Elementary Cellular Automata (ECA) dataset. Uses a contiguous-window asynchronous update scheme where s cells (a randomly placed block of width s) are updated per timestep. T=200 timesteps for richer signal. Tokens are 5-dimensional (includes did_change feature). Generated for ECANet-Skew models.

generation.token_feature_note: 5th feature is did_change (cell changed vs previous timestep)
generation.s_semantics: Number of cells updated per timestep. s=W (20) is fully synchronous; s=1 is maximally skewed.
generation.update_scheme: Contiguous block: a random start cell is chosen, and s cells starting from that position (mod W) are updated each step.

phase1.description: Fully synchronous update only (s=W=20). Clean rule inference baseline with no skew.

phase2.description: Stratified by (rule, s). Every rule appears at every s level exactly K times. Guaranteed balanced coverage.

field_descriptions.orbits: Raw ECA orbit over T=200 timesteps on a W=20 grid. Binary values {0, 1}.
field_descriptions.rule_bits: 8-bit binary representation of the ECA rule number (LSB first, bits 0-7).
field_descriptions.s_values: Number of cells updated per timestep for this sample. Integer in [1, 20].
field_descriptions.rule_ids: Integer rule number (0-255) corresponding to each sample.

file_format.storage_order: Rule-wise within each s group. All samples for rule 0 first, then rule 1, etc. Use DataLoader shuffle=True during training.

loading_example.python:
import numpy as np
orbits    = np.load('ECA_Data_Skew/phase2/train/orbits.npy')
rule_bits = np.load('ECA_Data_Skew/phase2/train/rule_bits.npy')
s_values  = np.load('ECA_Data_Skew/phase2/train/s_values.npy')
rule_ids  = np.load('ECA_Data_Skew/phase2/train/rule_ids.npy')

task.input: ECA orbit of shape [T, W] = [200, 20] with 5-dimensional tokens
task.target: 8-bit rule representation (rule_bits) or integer rule ID (rule_ids)
task.auxiliary_target: s value per sample (for phase2 skew-conditioned models)

comparison_notes.vs_ECANet_New: Same rule split (SEED=42), same W=20, but T=200 (vs T=100) and uses s-skew update (contiguous block) instead of alpha-async (independent Bernoulli per cell). Token dimensionality is 5 here vs 4 in ECANet-New.
